title: Shri Durga Mata Ki Aarti
intro: Read Shri Durga Mata Ki Aarti in Hindi and seek the divine blessings of Goddess Durga.
description:
Jai Ambe Gauri,
Maiya Jai Shyama Gauri.
Tumko nishidin dhyavat,
Hari Brahma Shiv ri॥
॥ Jai Ambe Gauri ॥

Maang sindoor virajat,
Teeko mrigmad ko.
Ujjwal se do naina,
Chandravadan neeko॥
॥ Jai Ambe Gauri ॥

Kanak saman kalevar,
Raktambar rajai.
Raktapushp gal mala,
Kanthan par sajai॥
॥ Jai Ambe Gauri ॥

Kehari vahan rajat,
Khadag khappar dhari.
Sur-nar munijan sevat,
Tinke dukh-hari॥
॥ Jai Ambe Gauri ॥

Kaanan kundal shobhit,
Nasagre moti.
Kotik chandra divakar,
Sam rajat jyoti॥
॥ Jai Ambe Gauri ॥

Shumbh-Nishumbh bidare,
Mahishasur ghati.
Dhoomra vilochana naina,
Nishidin madmati॥
॥ Jai Ambe Gauri ॥

Chand-Mund sanhare,
Shonitbeej hare.
Madhu-Kaitabh dou mare,
Sur bhay door kare॥
॥ Jai Ambe Gauri ॥

Brahmani, Rudrani,
Tum Kamala Rani.
Agam-Nigam bakhani,
Tum Shiv Patrani॥
॥ Jai Ambe Gauri ॥

Chaunsath yogini gaavat,
Nritya karat Bhairon.
Bajat taal mridanga,
Aru bajat damru॥
॥ Jai Ambe Gauri ॥

Tum hi jag ki mata,
Tum hi ho bharta.
Bhaktan ki dukh harta,
Sukh sampatti karta॥
॥ Jai Ambe Gauri ॥

Bhuja char ati shobhit,
Varmudra dhari.
Manvanchit phal paavat,
Sevat nar nari॥
॥ Jai Ambe Gauri ॥

Kanchan thaal virajat,
Agar kapoor bati.
Shri Malketu mein rajat,
Koti ratan jyoti॥
॥ Jai Ambe Gauri ॥

Shri Ambe ji ki aarti,
Jo koi nar gaave.
Kahat Shivanand Swami,
Sukh sampatti paave॥
॥ Jai Ambe Gauri ॥

faq.q1: What is the main purpose of performing Goddess Durga's Aarti?
faq.a1: Performing the Aarti of Goddess Durga invokes her fierce yet protective energy. Devotees believe it helps in overcoming internal and external obstacles, fears, and evil influences, granting them spiritual courage and confidence.
faq.q2: Which days are considered most auspicious for Durga Maa's Aarti?
faq.a2: While Durga Maa can be worshipped daily, Tuesdays and Fridays are specifically dedicated to her. Performing the Aarti during the nine days of Navratri is considered the most potent and auspicious time to receive her divine blessings.
faq.q3: What offerings (Prasad) does Goddess Durga prefer during Aarti?
faq.a3: Goddess Durga is fond of red flowers, especially Hibiscus (Gudhal). Offering homemade sweets like Halwa or Kheer, along with coconut and seasonal fruits, is considered an ideal way to please her during the Aarti.
faq.q4: Can Durga Maa's Aarti be performed at home?
faq.a4: You do not need a temple setting to perform the Aarti. Offering a ghee lamp and chanting the Aarti in front of her picture or idol with sincere devotion is equally effective and accepted at home.
faq.q5: What is the benefit of the 'Kshama Prarthana' after the Aarti?
faq.a5: The 'Kshama Prarthana' is a humble apology to the Goddess for any errors, lack of concentration, or procedural mistakes made during the Aarti. It cleanses the act of worship and ensures the devotee remains connected to her grace.
